amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: E. coli synthesizes purine and pyrimidine nucleotides de novo for DNA and RNA. Purine synthesis (10 steps) builds the ring on ribose-5-phosphate starting from PRPP, using amino acids (Gly, Gln, Asp) and one-carbon units from folate. Pyrimidine synthesis builds the ring first (UMP pathway, 6 steps), then attaches to ribose. Both pathways are regulated by feedback inhibition and transcriptional control. Salvage pathways recover preformed bases.

Nucleotide?} D -->|Purines| E[Purine Biosynthesis] D -->|Pyrimidines| F[Pyrimidine Biosynthesis] %% Purine Pathway Step-by-Step E --> G["PurF: PRPP + Gln → 5-Phosphoribosylamine"] G --> H["PurD: + Gly"] H --> I[GAR Glycinamide Ribotide] I --> J["PurN: + Formyl-THF"] J --> K(\1) K --> L["PurL: + Gln"] L --> M(\1) M --> N["PurM: Cyclization"] N --> O[AIR 5-Aminoimidazole Ribotide] O --> P["PurK: + CO2"] P --> Q(\1) Q --> R["PurE: + Asp"] R --> S(\1) S --> T["PurB: Release Fumarate"] T --> U(\1) U --> V["PurH: + Formyl-THF"] V --> W(\1) W --> X["PurH: Cyclization"] X --> Y[IMP Inosine Monophosphate] %% IMP Branch Point Y --> Z{OR: AMP
or GMP?} Z -->|PurA| AA[IMP + Asp + GTP → AMP] Z -->|PurB+GuaA| AB[IMP + NAD → GMP] AA --> AC[AMP Adenosine Monophosphate] AB --> AD[GMP Guanosine Monophosphate] %% Pyrimidine Pathway F --> AE["CarAB: Gln + CO2 + ATP → Carbamoyl-P"] AE --> AF["PyrB: Carbamoyl-P + Asp"] AF --> AG[Carbamoyl Aspartate] AG --> AH["PyrC: Cyclization"] AH --> AI[Dihydroorotate DHO] AI --> AJ["PyrD: DHO + NAD → Orotate"] AJ --> AK(\1) AK --> AL["PyrE: Orotate + PRPP → Orotidine-5-P"] AL --> AM[Orotidine Monophosphate] AM --> AN["PyrF: Decarboxylation"] AN --> AO[UMP Uridine Monophosphate] %% UMP to CTP AO --> AP["PyrH: UMP + ATP → UDP"] AP --> AQ(\1) AQ --> AR["Ndk: UDP + ATP → UTP"] AR --> AS(\1) AS --> AT["PyrG: UTP + Gln + ATP → CTP"] AT --> AU[CTP Cytidine Triphosphate] %% Nucleoside Diphosphate Kinase AC --> AV[AMP → ADP → ATP] AD --> AW[GMP → GDP → GTP] AV --> AX{AND: ATP

Levels?} %% Ribonucleotide Reductase AX -->|Sufficient| BB["RNR: ATP → dATP"] AY -->|Sufficient| BC["RNR: GTP → dGTP"] AZ -->|Sufficient| BD["RNR: UTP → dUTP"] BA -->|Sufficient| BE["RNR: CTP → dCTP"] BD --> BF["Dut: dUTP → dUMP"] BF --> BG["ThyA: dUMP + CH2-THF → dTMP"] BG --> BH[dTMP → dTDP → dTTP] %% DNA/RNA Synthesis BB --> BI(\1) BC --> BJ(\1) BE --> BK(\1) BH --> BL(\1) BI --> BM{OR: DNA
or RNA?} BJ --> BM BK --> BM BL --> BM AV --> BM AW --> BM AS --> BM AU --> BM BM -->|DNA| BN[DNA Polymerase] BM -->|RNA| BO[RNA Polymerase] BN --> BP([DNA Replication]) BO --> BQ([Transcription]) %% name: Nucleotide Biosynthesis (Purine and Pyrimidine Synthesis)
notes: Shows high OR gates (9) for purine/pyrimidine choice and AMP/GMP branch.
scientificAccuracy: Based on comprehensive biochemical characterization of E. coli nucleotide metabolism.

sources.title: Purine and Pyrimidine Nucleotide Biosynthesis and Its Control
Genetics and regulation of purine and pyrimidine biosynthesis in bacteria
Aspartate transcarbamylase of Escherichia coli: molecular weights and dissociation into catalytic and regulatory subunits
sources.authors: Gerhart, J. C., Schachman, H. K.
